evals.name: standalone-creation
evals.expected_output: Detects no handoff artifact (wip/vision_new-developer-portal_scope.md absent). Starts from Phase 1 (scope). Asks scoping questions about the project thesis, audience, and org fit. Produces a VISION doc at docs/visions/VISION-new-developer-portal.md with all 7 required sections in correct order. Thesis section is phrased as a hypothesis, not a problem statement.

evals.name: explore-handoff-detection
evals.expected_output: Detects existing handoff artifact at wip/vision_workflow-engine_scope.md. Skips Phase 1 (scope) and resumes at Phase 2 (discover). Uses the handoff artifact's problem statement, research leads, and decisions as input. Does not re-ask scoping questions that were already answered during /explore.

evals.name: visibility-gating-public
evals.expected_output: Detects Public visibility from CLAUDE.md. Produces a VISION doc with 7 required sections. Does NOT include Competitive Positioning or Resource Implications sections (private-only). All content is appropriate for public consumption.

evals.name: lifecycle-accept-verb
evals.expected_output: Detects lifecycle verb 'accept'. Validates Open Questions section is empty or removed. Runs transition-status.sh to transition from Draft to Accepted. Reports success. File stays in docs/visions/ (no directory movement for Accepted).

evals.name: lifecycle-sunset-with-move
evals.expected_output: Detects lifecycle verb 'sunset'. Runs transition-status.sh to transition from Active to Sunset. File is moved to docs/visions/sunset/VISION-old-project.md via git mv. Status updated in both frontmatter and body.

evals.name: jury-review-quality
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 spawns 3 jury agents focused on vision-specific quality. Agents check: thesis is a hypothesis (not problem statement), success criteria are project-level (not feature-level), org fit explains why HERE, non-goals are about identity (not scope), content boundaries are respected (no features, sequencing, or architecture).

evals.name: empty-argument-prompts
evals.expected_output: With no argument, skill asks the user what project they want to create a vision for. Does not assume a topic or start drafting immediately.

evals.name: crystallize-discrimination
evals.prompt: /explore --auto --strategic should we build a developer analytics dashboard
evals.expected_output: Exploration in strategic scope. Crystallize framework scores VISION higher than PRD because: project doesn't exist yet, exploration centered on 'should we build this', org fit is the core question. VISION signals fire, PRD anti-signals fire (no existing project). Routes to /vision via Phase 5 handoff.
